description: The capacity to exert force or withstand pressure; power available for action. 'Puro' names what enables—the resource that makes difficult things possible.
sound_symbolism: The word gathers with power. 'Pu' pushes with concentrated force, strength gathering. 'Ro' grounds with solid stability, power rooted.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'mia puro' (my strength). What enables 'meloa' (persevere). Related to 'kenua' (energy) as capacity to force, and to 'kema' (strong), its adjective counterpart.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Inner strength—the fortitude that comes from equanimity, not aggression.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Strength through practice—power built through sustained effort, not given.
